sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Sager traction splint is a medial traction device that can be useful in selected femoral shaft fractures and has design advantages in some bilateral-femur scenarios. As with any traction splint, the injury pattern must suit the device before application.

sections.title: Application
sections.content:
1. Confirm a traction splint is indicated and provide analgesia

2. Position the splint medially according to device design, ensuring the proximal support is correctly placed

3. Secure the distal attachment and apply steady traction until pain and alignment improve

4. Secure the remaining straps and reassess neurovascular status and patient comfort

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- The Sager design can offer options in some bilateral femur fracture situations, but only within training and protocol
- Reassess traction effect frequently during movement and transport
- Apply the same general traction-splint contraindications used for other femoral traction devices
